steps.reveal: Hodierno die = on this present day (abl. of time)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'hodiernus dies' — 'Hodierno die' is ablative of time when
hints.accusative: Accusative of time expresses duration — 'Hodierno die' is ablative expressing a point in time
hints.dative: Dative would be 'hodierno diei' — this is ablative of time when

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'diei' ends in -ei

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'urbs' — the -em ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'urbe' — 'urbem' with -em is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'urbis' — 'urbem' is accusative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'urbis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'urbis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'urbis' ends in -is

steps.prompt: What does the preposition 'ex' govern here?

hints.accusative: 'Ex' always takes the ablative, never the accusative
hints.genitive: 'Ex' governs the ablative — genitive is not used with 'ex'
hints.dative: 'Ex' governs the ablative — dative is not used with 'ex'

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'summum periculum' — the -o endings mark ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'summum periculum' — 'summo periculo' is ablative
hints.dative: Dative singular of 2nd declension also ends in -o, but 'ex' requires the ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'periculi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'periculi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'periculi' ends in -i

hints.to destroy: 'To destroy' is 'delere' — 'eripui' comes from 'eripere' (to snatch away)

steps.reveal: eripui = I snatched away / I rescued

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'eripio' — 'eripui' with the -ui perfect stem is perfect
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'eripueram' — 'eripui' is perfect indicative
hints.perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'eripuerim' — 'eripui' is perfect indicative

hints.rapio, rapere, rapui, raptum: These are the parts of 'rapere' (to seize) — the base verb without the e- prefix
hints.erigo, erigere, erexi, erectum: These are the parts of 'erigere' (to raise up) — different verb
hints.eruo, eruere, erui, erutum: These are the parts of 'eruere' (to dig out) — different verb

steps.prompt: What type of ablative construction is 'ex summo periculo'?

hints.ablative of means: Ablative of means answers 'by what instrument?' — 'ex periculo' expresses separation from danger
hints.ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires a participle or noun + participle — this is 'ex' + ablative
hints.ablative of manner: Ablative of manner tells how — 'ex periculo' tells from what the city was rescued

translation: On this day I rescued the city from the greatest danger
